# PVMath Master Context

**Single source of truth for the entire PVMath platform.** Generated by full project audit (codebase + git history + live website + existing memory files) on 2026-06-18. Supersedes stale sections of `CLAUDE.md` and `PVMath_Project_Context.md` where they conflict with this document — see each section for what changed and why. Those two files are not deleted; this one is the one to trust first.

---

## 1. Executive Summary

**What PVMath is:** PVMath-Solar Site Intelligence Platform for **ground-mount solar only** — Fixed Tilt and Single-Axis Tracker, Standard and Agri-PV (dual-use). No rooftop, carport, floating, or BIPV.

**Mission:** Automate the slow, manual parts of solar project development — from first site coordinates to actionable engineering data — turning what used to take days into a screening that takes minutes.

**Target users:** Solar EPCs, project developers, and engineering firms, worldwide (explicitly not DACH-only — "built with European markets in mind, but global data coverage from day one").

**Current development status (as of 2026-06-18, 7 days after first commit on 2026-06-11):**
- Three modules are live and public: **SiteIQ**, **TerrainIQ**, **YieldIQ**.
- A fourth module, **LayoutIQ** (auto layout + BOM generator), is functionally built but gated admin-only — not yet released to real users.
- Paid pricing is live: Free / Professional (€199/mo) / Developer (€799/mo) / Enterprise (custom).
- Stripe billing is **not** actually wired up yet (placeholder link).
- Auth, multi-project save, and PDF reporting all work in production on Railway.
- Development pace is very fast — dozens of commits per day during active sessions. This document will go stale quickly; treat git history and live code as more authoritative than any narrative summary, including this one.

---

## 2. Company & Branding

- **Company:** PVMath
- **Founder/Owner:** Mohammed Ismail Pasha — solo founder, side project alongside a full-time job as Solar Engineer / Product Owner at Ideematec GmbH, Regensburg, Germany. **Does not write code directly — all changes are made by Claude.**
- **Legal entity (per `impressum.html`):** Currently a **sole proprietorship under the owner's own name**, not a registered company — "Anbieter: Mohammed Ismail Pasha, Straubinger Strasse 1, 93055 Regensburg, Deutschland." No GmbH or other formal entity exists for PVMath itself. This matters for the Developer-tier promise of "custom invoice & VAT billing" — confirm legal/tax setup before that's truly deliverable (see §14).
- **Domains owned:** pvmath.com (primary website), pvmath.de, pvmath.eu (both linked from the website contact section, presumably redirecting or reserved)
- **Email addresses:** `contact@pvmath.com` (canonical, public-facing — `contact@pvmath.de` was fully replaced site-wide on 2026-06-17), owner's personal email `ismailpasha747@gmail.com`, plus `ismail.p@pvmath.de` (both are in the app's admin allowlist `_ADMIN` in `app.py`)
- **Phone:** +49 15 901 482 999 (Impressum only)
- **Social media:** **None found anywhere in the codebase or live site.** No LinkedIn, Twitter/X, Instagram, or Facebook links exist on pvmath.com or in the app. A LinkedIn company-page banner asset was designed this session (see below) in anticipation of a LinkedIn presence, but no company page is confirmed to exist yet — see §14.
- **Branding guidelines:**
  - Light theme everywhere on the marketing site and Streamlit content area (`base = "light"`, background `#f5f7f5`, primary `#1d9e52`, text `#1a2e1a`) — **dark theme is explicitly rejected** per owner preference.
  - The in-app **sidebar** is intentionally dark green (`#0d1a0d` background, `#1d9e52` active state) — this is a deliberate contrast against the light main content area, not an inconsistency.
  - Typography: Inter (sidebar/app chrome), system sans-serif elsewhere.
  - Logo: green rounded-square badge with white "PV" wordmark; full wordmark logo also exists (`assets/logo-wordmark.svg/.png`).
  - Terminology: use "Standard Ground Mount" or "Agri-PV", never the German "Freifläche" in user-facing copy.
- **Current taglines:**
  - Brand tagline: **"From site to system."** (must render non-italic — explicit, repeated instruction)
  - Hero tagline: "⚡ Don't wait for the topo survey — get import-ready DXF & LandXML terrain files in minutes, not weeks."
  - Footer tagline: "Solar site intelligence tools for EPCs, developers, financiers and everyone shaping solar projects."
  - Positioning lines: "From the field. Not a desk." / "Purpose-Built for Solar" / "Built in Germany"
- **Assets on hand:** `assets/logo.svg`, `logo-192.png`, `logo-512.png`, `logo-wordmark.svg/.png`, `favicon.ico/-16/-32`, `pvmath-press-kit.zip` (downloadable from website footer). A LinkedIn banner (1128×191 px logical, lush green terrain illustration with solar panels, contour-dot terrain texture, PV logo badge, "PVMath" wordmark, "Solar Site Intelligence Platform" tagline, non-italic "From site to system." subtext) was generated this session via a direct pycairo render (SVG-to-raster tooling in the sandbox was broken — see `PVMath_Project_Context.md` history if resurrected). **This PNG was delivered to the user directly and was never committed to the repo** — it's a design asset, not application code. A broken, abandoned `.svg` intermediate (`assets/linkedin-banner.svg`) from a failed earlier rendering attempt is still sitting in the repo as harmless clutter (see §9).

---

## 3. Architecture Overview

**High-level shape:** A single Streamlit Python application is both the "frontend" and "backend" — server-rendered, stateful via `st.session_state`, no separate API/SPA split. A completely separate static website (plain HTML/CSS/JS) handles marketing/sales and is the only part of the stack with no Python in it.

- **Frontend:** Streamlit UI (`app.py` + `pages/*.py`), styled with injected CSS/JS (`pvmath_styles.py`, inline `st.markdown(..., unsafe_allow_html=True)` blocks). Maps rendered via `streamlit-folium`/`folium`. Multi-page routing via `st.navigation(_pages, position="hidden")` — Streamlit's own nav UI is hidden and replaced with a hand-built sidebar, because the native control was low-contrast and inconsistent across Streamlit versions.
- **Backend:** Same Streamlit process — no separate API server. Business logic lives directly inside `pages/*.py` and helper modules (`pvmath_auth.py`, `pvmath_styles.py`).
- **APIs (all called server-side from within the Streamlit process):** PVGIS (solar resource + yield), OpenTopoData / Copernicus GLO-30 (terrain), Nominatim/OSM (geocoding), Supabase REST (auth + data), Brevo HTTP API (email).
- **Hosting:** Railway (one service, nixpacks build) for the app; GitHub Pages for the static website. These are two completely independent deploys with no shared infrastructure.
- **Deployment strategy:** Push to `main` on GitHub → Railway auto-deploys the app via its GitHub integration; the same push also updates `index.html` on GitHub Pages if it changed. A `staging` branch exists as a **git workflow convention only** — there is no separate staging *deployment*; everything that lands on `main` is immediately live to real users.
- **Data flow (typical user session):**
  1. User signs in (Supabase auth, `pvmath_auth.py`) → auth gate (`render_auth_page`) blocks all anonymous access.
  2. User sets up a project on `pages/project.py` — Quick Mode (pin drop) or Full Mode (drawn boundary) — choosing project name, country, land use, mounting type.
  3. On "Save Project," the project dict is written to `st.session_state["pvm_project"]` and persisted to Supabase (`user_projects` table, multi-row per user since the 2026-06-16 migration).
  4. The user opens a module (SiteIQ / TerrainIQ / YieldIQ / LayoutIQ) via a "Continue" button on the Project page — **not** via sidebar links, which were deliberately removed to stop users from jumping into a module before a boundary draft was actually saved.
  5. The module reads the shared project context, calls the relevant external API(s), renders results, and (for SiteIQ/TerrainIQ/YieldIQ/LayoutIQ) builds a downloadable PDF via ReportLab.
  6. The Supabase refresh token is kept in both `st.session_state` and a `?s=` URL query param, re-asserted as the very last line of every `app.py` run, because Streamlit's own multipage router can silently strip query params on navigation — without this, every refresh logged users out.

---

## 4. Tech Stack

**Languages:** Python (app), HTML/CSS/vanilla JS (marketing website, single `index.html` with an EN/DE i18n system via `data-i18n` attributes + a JS `T` translation object)

**Frameworks:** Streamlit

**Libraries (`requirements.txt`):**
```
streamlit
requests
pandas
reportlab
pillow
folium
streamlit-folium
supabase       # listed, but NOT actually used as an SDK — see below
numpy
scipy
ezdxf          # DXF read/write, used by LayoutIQ
shapely        # geometry ops (boundary setback, polygons), used by LayoutIQ
matplotlib
```
Note: `pvmath_auth.py` talks to Supabase via raw `requests` calls to its REST API directly — the `supabase-py` SDK in `requirements.txt` is **not actually imported anywhere** (it was tried and reverted early on, per commit `4e34491`, "Replace supabase-py with direct requests — fix DNS error").

**Railway configuration (`railway.toml`):**
```toml
[build]
builder = "nixpacks"

[deploy]
startCommand = "streamlit run app.py --server.port $PORT --server.address 0.0.0.0"
healthcheckPath = "/_stcore/health"
healthcheckTimeout = 300
restartPolicyType = "on_failure"
```

**Streamlit theme (`.streamlit/config.toml`):** light theme, primary `#1d9e52`, background `#f5f7f5`, secondary background `#eef2ee`, text `#1a2e1a`.

**Environment variables / secrets (set in Railway → Variables, production):**
| Variable | Used for |
|---|---|
| `SUPABASE_URL` | Auth + DB REST calls (`pvmath_auth.py`) |
| `SUPABASE_KEY` | Auth + DB REST calls (anon/public key) |
| `BREVO_API_KEY` | OTP/notification email via Brevo HTTP API |
| `SMTP_HOST`, `SMTP_PORT`, `SMTP_USER`, `SMTP_PASS`, `SMTP_FROM` | SMTP fallback, mainly for local/dev email |

`os.environ` is checked **before** `st.secrets` everywhere (deliberate ordering fix — Railway only provides env vars, not `st.secrets`). A local `.streamlit/secrets.toml` exists for local dev only, is gitignored, and was explicitly removed from git history (commit `f17bde6`, "remove secrets.toml from git — Railway reads from env vars only").

`STRIPE_LINK` is **not** an env var — it's a hardcoded constant in `pvmath_auth.py`, currently still the placeholder `https://buy.stripe.com/YOUR_LINK_HERE`.

**Third-party integrations:** Supabase (auth + Postgres, direct REST, no SDK), Brevo (transactional email — OTP currently disabled pending account activation), SMTP (dev fallback), PVGIS JRC (`PVcalc` endpoint), OpenTopoData (`eudem25m`/`srtm30m` + Copernicus GLO-30 terrarium tiles), Nominatim/OpenStreetMap (geocoding), Formspree (website contact form only, form ID `xpwzgkvo`), Stripe (referenced, not yet integrated).

---

## 5. Module Status

### SiteIQ — Module 01 — **Live, public**
- **Purpose:** Fast solar site pre-screening for any location globally.
- **Inputs:** Coordinates (pin drop, address geocode, Google Maps URL/coordinate paste, KML/KMZ upload), project country, land use (Standard/Agri-PV), mounting type (Fixed Tilt/Tracker), site area.
- **Outputs:** Annual/monthly GHI & irradiation (PVGIS), terrain/slope overview, flood-risk indicator, country-aware regulatory flags, capacity estimate (MW via density table), overall verdict, client-ready PDF report.
- **Implementation:** `pages/siteiq.py` (1,514 lines). PVGIS `PVcalc` with `raddatabase` auto-pick (PVGIS-SARAH2), falling back to PVGIS-ERA5 where SARAH2 doesn't cover (India, the Americas, Australia/Oceania). Terrain dataset chosen by lat/lon bounding box (`eudem25m` for Europe, `srtm30m` globally).
- **Known limitations:** Regional solar-database coverage gaps (mitigated by fallback, not eliminated); flood risk is an elevation-based heuristic, not authoritative flood-zone data.
- **Free tier:** 5 screenings/month.

### TerrainIQ — Module 02 — **Live, public**
- **Purpose:** Satellite terrain extraction for solar site engineering — CAD-ready terrain export.
- **Inputs:** Drawn site boundary on a satellite map, or uploaded KML/KMZ/DXF.
- **Outputs:** Elevation & solar-grade slope heatmaps, contour lines at custom spacing, DXF and LandXML terrain file export (Civil 3D-ready), PDF terrain report with a slope distribution table.
- **Implementation:** `pages/terrainiq.py` (1,405 lines). Pulls Copernicus GLO-30 DEM (~30 m native, resampled to a 5 m grid) via OpenTopoData terrarium tiles (`deg2tile`/`tile2deg`/`fetch_terrarium_tile`/`get_dem_for_bbox`). Exports via `export_xyz`, `export_landxml`, `export_dxf`.
- **Known limitations:** Small-area grids previously crashed (fixed); DEM tile alignment had a sentinel-value bug (fixed, elevation map dropped in favor of slope-map-only as part of that fix).
- **A root-level duplicate `terrainiq.py` exists but is dead code** — not imported anywhere; do not edit it expecting any effect on the live app.

### YieldIQ — Module 03 — **Live, public**
- **Purpose:** Quick pre-layout energy yield estimation, before committing to full PV layout design.
- **Inputs:** Coordinates, DC capacity, GCR (1P/2P configurations), tracker vs. fixed-tilt selection, soiling/other loss assumptions.
- **Outputs:** Specific yield (kWh/kWp), performance ratio, capacity factor, monthly energy profile, POA irradiance, tracker-vs-fixed comparison, preliminary yield PDF.
- **Implementation:** `pages/yieldiq.py` (1,106 lines). Uses PVGIS `PVcalc` — **important gotcha:** `PVcalc` has no `trackingtype` parameter; tracker yield must be modeled via the `inclined_axis` system type instead (an earlier bug sent `trackingtype: 1`, which PVGIS silently ignored).
- **Known limitations:** Explicitly disclaimed on the website as "early-stage feasibility only — not a substitute for bankable energy assessment (PVsyst, DNV SolarFarmer, Aurora)."
- **Inconsistency to fix:** the website's module card for YieldIQ still has CSS class `"soon"` even though its pill text reads "Live Now" — visually it should match SiteIQ/TerrainIQ's `"live"` class. See §9.

### LayoutIQ — Module 04 — **Admin-only, in progress, not public**
- **Purpose:** Auto PV layout + BOM (bill of materials) generator.
- **Inputs:** Site polygon (KML, DXF in local/projected metres, or pasted lat/lon coordinates), module dimensions, string/inverter configuration, mounting type (Fixed Tilt or Single-Axis Tracker — SAT rows run N–S with E–W pitch, azimuth hidden for SAT).
- **Outputs:** Generated row layout with boundary setback (via `shapely` buffer), layout drawing, computed BOM, PDF report.
- **Implementation:** `pages/_layoutiq.py` (772 lines, **leading underscore is intentional** — excludes it from the normal `st.navigation` page list; only appended to the nav for emails in `app.py`'s `_ADMIN` set: `ismailpasha747@gmail.com`, `ismail.p@pvmath.de`).
- **Status:** Functionally complete code exists and has had real iteration (e.g., Fixed Tilt vs. SAT handling), but per the project's launch sequencing rule, modules go live one at a time only once stable — LayoutIQ has not yet been promoted from admin-only to public.
- **Known limitations:** DXF input must be in local/projected metres (UTM or similar) — lat/lon DXF isn't supported; use the paste-coordinates method instead.

### Overview / My Projects / Project — supporting pages, **live**
- Not "modules" in the marketing sense, but core shared infrastructure: project setup (Quick/Full Mode), multi-project dashboard, and a stats overview. All three modules above read from the project context these pages establish and save.
- Multi-project support required a Supabase schema migration (`supabase_migration_multi_project.sql`) — the original `user_projects` table had `user_id` as its primary key, limiting every user to exactly one saved project ever; the migration adds a proper `id` primary key so users can save multiple projects.

### Modules referenced only in older planning docs — status unconfirmed
`CLAUDE.md`'s original roadmap listed **RevenueIQ** (EEG/feed-in tariff calculator), **ProcureIQ**, and **FieldIQ** as the next planned modules after SiteIQ. None of these appear anywhere in the current codebase or live website — the actual build instead went straight to TerrainIQ → YieldIQ → LayoutIQ as modules 2–4. It's unclear whether RevenueIQ/ProcureIQ/FieldIQ were abandoned, deprioritized, or simply haven't come up yet in conversation. **Needs owner confirmation — see §14.**

---

## 6. API Documentation

### PVGIS (EU Joint Research Centre)
- **Endpoint:** `https://re.jrc.ec.europa.eu/api/v5_2/PVcalc`
- **Used for:** Solar resource data (GHI/DNI/DHI), specific yield, POA irradiance, tracker-vs-fixed-tilt yield comparison.
- **Auth:** None — public API.
- **Rate limits:** Not officially documented by PVGIS; no explicit client-side throttling implemented.
- **Error handling:** `raddatabase` defaults to auto-pick (PVGIS-SARAH2); code explicitly falls back to `PVGIS-ERA5` for regions SARAH2 doesn't cover (India, the Americas, Australia/Oceania). On total failure, SiteIQ shows "⚠️ Data unavailable" rather than fabricating a misleading "0 kWh/m² — Poor" result.
- **Implementation notes:** Calls use `peakpower=1 kWp` for normalized per-kWp yield math. `PVcalc` has **no `trackingtype` parameter** — tracker modeling must use the `inclined_axis` system type, not a tracking flag (a previously-fixed bug sent the now-ignored `trackingtype: 1`).

### OpenTopoData
- **Endpoint pattern:** `https://api.opentopodata.org/v1/{dataset}`
- **Used for:** SiteIQ's quick terrain/slope check (`eudem25m` for Europe — 34–72°N, −25–45°E — `srtm30m` globally); TerrainIQ's higher-resolution work instead pulls Copernicus GLO-30 DEM directly via terrarium tile URLs (not through this REST endpoint).
- **Auth:** None — public API.
- **Rate limits:** Not documented or explicitly handled in code.
- **Error handling:** Dataset selection is by lat/lon bounding box only (unlike `assess_eeg()`, which prioritizes a `project_country` text field — these two features deliberately use different precedence logic; don't assume they're consistent).

### Nominatim / OpenStreetMap
- **Used for:** Address-to-coordinate geocoding.
- **Auth:** None required, but a descriptive `User-Agent` header is mandatory: `"SiteIQ/1.0 (pvmath.com; contact@pvmath.com)"`. A generic User-Agent gets blocked by Nominatim's usage policy — this has happened before; never revert it.
- **Rate limits:** Nominatim's published policy is ~1 request/second; not explicitly throttled client-side, relying on current low traffic volume.
- **Error handling:** Falls back to manual pin drop or coordinate paste if geocoding fails.

### Supabase REST API
- **Used for:** Auth (sign-up, sign-in, token refresh, password reset) and data (project CRUD, usage tracking) — direct `requests` calls, **not** the `supabase-py` SDK.
- **Auth:** `SUPABASE_URL` + `SUPABASE_KEY` (anon/public key) env vars.
- **Rate limits:** Whatever the Supabase project's tier defaults to — not customized.
- **Error handling:** `_parse_err()` helper in `pvmath_auth.py` extracts a readable message from Supabase's REST error responses.

### Brevo HTTP API
- **Used for:** OTP / transactional email (primary path), with SMTP as fallback.
- **Auth:** `BREVO_API_KEY` env var.
- **Status:** **OTP verification is currently disabled** pending Brevo account activation — the app auto-logs in users right after signup instead of requiring email verification. Re-enable once Brevo is confirmed active.

### Formspree
- **Used for:** The marketing website's contact form only — not part of the Streamlit app.
- **Form ID:** `xpwzgkvo` (`action="https://formspree.io/f/xpwzgkvo"`).

### Stripe
- **Status:** Referenced but not integrated. `STRIPE_LINK` in `pvmath_auth.py` is a placeholder (`https://buy.stripe.com/YOUR_LINK_HERE`). The sidebar's "Manage Membership" button links here but currently goes nowhere functional. By design, no plan/price/upgrade logic is hardcoded elsewhere in the UI — that's deferred to the real Stripe Customer Portal once this link is live.

---

## 7. Deployment Documentation

- **Railway setup:** Single Railway service, `nixpacks` builder, GitHub-integrated auto-deploy on every push to `main`.
- **Build process:** Handled by nixpacks (no custom Dockerfile).
- **Startup command:** `streamlit run app.py --server.port $PORT --server.address 0.0.0.0`
- **Healthcheck:** `GET /_stcore/health`, 300s timeout, restart policy `on_failure`.
- **Production environment:** One Railway service answers for **two** custom domains — `siteiq.pvmath.com` and `topoiq.pvmath.com` — both true reverse-proxy CNAMEs (Railway terminates the connection directly; no redirect, no stripped query strings).
- **Domain mappings / DNS** (registrar: **Namecheap**):

| Type | Host | Value | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| A | @ | 185.199.108.153, .109.153, .110.153, .111.153 | pvmath.com → GitHub Pages (website) |
| CNAME | www | ismailpvmath.github.io | website |
| CNAME | siteiq | `<railway-generated-host>` | app, reverse proxy |
| CNAME | terrainiq | `<railway-generated-host>` (different host, same deployment) | app, reverse proxy |
| TXT | _railway-verify.siteiq / _railway-verify.terrainiq | Railway verification tokens | domain verification |

- **GitHub repo:** `IsmailPVMath/siteiq`, branches `main` (production) and `staging` (pre-merge workflow only — **not** a separate deployment).
- **Common deployment failures / gotchas:**
  - `st.secrets` vs `os.environ` precedence had to be explicitly fixed (`os.environ` first, `st.secrets` fallback) — Railway only injects real environment variables, it doesn't populate `st.secrets`.
  - `secrets.toml` must never be committed — it was removed from git history once already (commit `f17bde6`) and stays gitignored; Railway reads only from its own Variables panel in production.
  - **Sandbox git commits leave stale lock files on the real filesystem.** When changes are committed from inside a Claude sandbox session, the sandbox's own cleanup of its git temp/lock files fails with "Operation not permitted" — this leaves real `.git/HEAD.lock`, `.git/index.lock`, and `.git/objects/*/tmp_obj_*` files sitting in the actual `~/Desktop/solarscout/.git` folder on the owner's Mac, which then block the owner's *own* next local `git checkout`/`merge`/etc. until manually deleted (`rm -f .git/HEAD.lock .git/index.lock`). This has happened repeatedly. **Recommended fix going forward: the owner runs `git add`/`git commit`/`git push` locally themselves**, rather than Claude running them from the sandbox.
  - **The sandbox cannot reach github.com at all** — not just push, but `git fetch` also fails with `Received HTTP code 403 from proxy after CONNECT`. Any verification of what's actually on `origin/main` or `origin/staging` must come from the owner running `git log` locally and pasting the output — it cannot be confirmed from inside the sandbox.

---

## 8. Folder Structure

```
solarscout/                          (repo root = local dev folder = ~/Desktop/solarscout)
├── CLAUDE.md                        — long-form project memory (partially stale, see notes throughout this doc)
├── PVMath_Project_Context.md        — short operational checklist for Claude sessions (mostly accurate, a few stale points — e.g. it predates the `staging` branch workflow)
├── PVMath_Master_Context.md         — this file
├── PVMath_Launch_Business_Guide.docx — business strategy reference (not deep-audited this pass)
├── app.py                           — main Streamlit entrypoint: page config, auth gate, sidebar, navigation, bfcache/token-refresh fixes
├── pvmath_auth.py                   — Supabase auth (REST, no SDK), OTP/email, usage tracking, project save/load, paywall UI, Stripe link
├── pvmath_styles.py                 — shared CSS/JS injected into every module page (typography, Streamlit-chrome removal)
├── terrainiq.py                        — DEAD CODE, root-level duplicate, not imported anywhere
├── usage_tracker.py                 — DEAD CODE, root-level local-JSON usage tracker, superseded by Supabase-backed usage functions in pvmath_auth.py
├── requirements.txt
├── railway.toml                     — Railway build/deploy config
├── .streamlit/
│   ├── config.toml                  — Streamlit theme (light, green accent)
│   └── secrets.toml                 — gitignored, local dev only
├── .gitignore
├── pages/                           — Streamlit multi-page app pages
│   ├── overview.py                  — stats/dashboard landing page
│   ├── my_projects.py               — multi-project list/management
│   ├── project.py                   — project setup (Quick/Full Mode), module hand-off buttons
│   ├── siteiq.py                    — Module 01, live
│   ├── terrainiq.py                    — Module 02, live
│   ├── yieldiq.py                   — Module 03, live
│   └── _layoutiq.py                 — Module 04, admin-only (leading underscore = hidden from normal nav)
├── assets/                          — logos, favicons, press kit, LinkedIn banner debris
├── index.html                       — marketing website (single file, EN/DE i18n, pricing, modules, contact form)
├── index.html.bak                   — backup copy, untracked clutter
├── impressum.html / privacy.html / terms.html — legal pages (German Impressum is legally primary, English translation below it)
├── sitemap.xml
├── CNAME                            — GitHub Pages custom domain file, contains "pvmath.com"
└── supabase_migration_multi_project.sql — schema migration enabling multiple saved projects per user
```

## 9. Bug Tracker

### Fixed (representative — see `git log` for the complete day-by-day record; this project moves fast)
- Sidebar vanishing on browser Back/Refresh (bfcache) — fixed via a `pageshow`/`popstate` forced-reload script plus a localStorage-backed refresh-token self-heal.
- "Refresh logs you out" — Streamlit's multipage router was silently stripping the Supabase refresh token from the URL on navigation; fixed by re-asserting `st.query_params["s"]` as the literal last line of every `app.py` run.
- PDF table cells overflowing — ReportLab plain strings don't wrap; fixed by wrapping every cell in a `Paragraph`.
- PDF emojis rendering as black squares — fixed by using colored `Paragraph` text instead of emoji glyphs.
- YieldIQ tracker yield was wrong — `PVcalc` has no `trackingtype` param; fixed to use the `inclined_axis` system type.
- TerrainIQ DEM tile alignment / sentinel-value bug — fixed; elevation map was dropped in favor of slope-map-only as part of the same fix.
- Nominatim geocoding silently blocked — generic User-Agent replaced with a descriptive one (`SiteIQ/1.0 (pvmath.com; contact@pvmath.com)`).
- Non-DACH sites showing German regulatory authorities — `assess_eeg()` fixed to prioritize a `project_country` text field over lat/lon bounding box.
- `NameError`: `site_name` → `project_name` rename mismatch in a download button.
- PVGIS "Optimal Tilt: True°" — was reading the `optimal` boolean key instead of the `value` key.
- Multiple sidebar visibility/contrast bugs across browsers (native Streamlit auto-collapse fighting the custom toggle, `display:none` vs `visibility:hidden` semantics) — fixed with layered `!important` CSS and a redundant fail-safe "Show sidebar" button rendered outside the sidebar's own DOM subtree.
- ReportLab `colWidths` crash — `"Ncm"` strings aren't valid values; fixed to `N*cm`.
- **Recurring stale git lock files from sandbox commits blocking the owner's local git operations** — root-caused 2026-06-18 (see §7); not a code bug, an environment limitation. Process fix: run git commands locally, not from the sandbox.

### Open bugs / known technical debt
- `STRIPE_LINK` is still a placeholder — "Manage Membership" doesn't yet show a real plan or upgrade path. Needs the owner's own Stripe account access; Claude cannot complete this part.
- Brevo OTP verification is disabled pending account activation — users are currently auto-logged-in right after signup with no email verification step enforced.
- **Website inconsistency:** YieldIQ's module card in `index.html` still has the CSS class `"soon"` even though its pill reads "Live Now" — cosmetic mismatch, should be `"live"` to match SiteIQ/TerrainIQ.
- Root-level `terrainiq.py` and `usage_tracker.py` are dead code — confirmed unused via import search, but still sitting in the repo. Harmless, but worth deleting eventually to avoid future confusion about which file is "real."
- `assets/linkedin-banner.svg` is a broken, abandoned intermediate file from a failed SVG-rendering attempt this session — the real deliverable was a `.png` rendered via pycairo and was never committed. Couldn't be deleted from the sandbox (permission error); still there, unreferenced and harmless.
- `.DS_Store` (root and `assets/`) and `index.html.bak` are untracked clutter, not in `.gitignore`.
- There is no real staging *deployment* — only a `staging` git branch used as a pre-merge workflow step. Everything merged to `main` goes live on Railway immediately.

---

## 10. Product Roadmap

**Completed:** SiteIQ, TerrainIQ, YieldIQ (all live and public) · Supabase auth with password reset · multi-project save/list/delete · PDF reporting for all three live modules · EN/DE i18n across the website and app · custom domains for both app subdomains and the marketing site · tiered pricing page (Free/Professional/Developer/Enterprise) · shared Project Setup flow (Quick Mode / Full Mode) feeding all modules.

**In Progress:** LayoutIQ (admin-only, functionally built, pending public launch per the one-module-at-a-time rule) · Brevo OTP re-activation · Stripe Customer Portal integration.

**Planned (stated on the live website):** API access for the Developer tier ("Q4 2026") · white-label reports, SLA, and dedicated onboarding for Enterprise.

**Future ideas / unconfirmed status:** RevenueIQ, ProcureIQ, FieldIQ — present only in the older `CLAUDE.md` roadmap, with zero trace in current code or the live website's module list. Needs explicit confirmation from the owner on whether these are still planned (see §14).

---

## 11. Business Strategy

- **Target customers:** Solar EPCs, project developers, and engineering firms, explicitly worldwide rather than DACH-only.
- **Pricing — current (live as of 2026-06-18):**

| Tier | Price | Limits / Features |
|---|---|---|
| Free | €0/mo | 5 analyses/module/month, PDF + DXF/LandXML export, no priority refresh or support |
| Professional ("Most Popular") | €199/mo | 50 analyses/module/month, full module access, priority data refresh, 1-business-day email support |
| Developer | €799/mo | 250 analyses/module/month, up to 5 team seats, API access (Q4 2026), shared workspace, priority support, custom invoicing & VAT billing |
| Enterprise | Custom | Unlimited analyses, custom seats, SLA, white-label reports, API, dedicated onboarding |

VAT (19%) is added for EU customers. **Pricing history** (illustrates how fast this is moving): launched at a flat €99 (2026-06-12) → restructured to Free / Pro €149 / Dev €499 / Enterprise (2026-06-15) → raised to Pro €199 / Dev €799 (2026-06-18, current), the latter increase explicitly justified by the owner as a response to early user demand/feedback, not a cost change.

- **Competitor references found in product copy:** PVsyst, DNV SolarFarmer, Aurora — explicitly named in YieldIQ's disclaimer to position it as a fast pre-screening complement, not a bankable-assessment replacement. No other competitor analysis was found in the codebase itself.
- **Market positioning:** "From the field. Not a desk." — leans on founder-market fit (a hands-on utility-scale solar engineer building the tool, not a generalist SaaS team); "Purpose-Built for Solar, not a generic GIS tool adapted for solar."
- **Supplementary reference:** `PVMath_Launch_Business_Guide.docx` exists in the repo root and likely contains additional strategy detail beyond what's reflected in code/copy — it was not deep-audited in this pass (no text-extraction tool was available in the sandbox at the time). Worth a dedicated read-through next time business strategy is being revised.

---

## 12. Decision Log

Chronological, key decisions only — see `git log` for the exhaustive day-by-day record (project started 2026-06-11; this log already spans 7 days and ~150 commits).

- **2026-06-11 — First commit.** SiteIQ built first: project type selector, interactive map, KML/KMZ import, country-aware regulatory + terrain logic, global (not just-Europe) data sources from day one.
- **2026-06-12 — Added TerrainIQ (Module 2)** and Supabase-backed auth + a paywall, with an initial flat €99 price. Decided early that pricing/paywall infrastructure should exist before scaling features.
- **2026-06-12/13 — Hosting decision: moved to Railway.** Streamlit Community Cloud couldn't provide a true custom top-level domain (only a `*.streamlit.app` subdomain), which was a hard requirement for `siteiq.pvmath.com`/`topoiq.pvmath.com`.
- **2026-06-13 — Replaced `supabase-py` SDK with direct REST `requests` calls** after the SDK caused a DNS error in the deployed environment — decided to keep the dependency surface minimal and debuggable.
- **2026-06-14 — YieldIQ built and made public as Module 03**, after briefly being hidden again the same day pending stability, then re-confirmed live. Decision: 3 live modules (SiteIQ/TerrainIQ/YieldIQ), not the RevenueIQ path originally sketched in early planning.
- **2026-06-14 — LayoutIQ (Module 04) added, admin-only from day one** — explicit decision to never expose an unstable module to real users, gated by an email allowlist rather than a feature flag in code.
- **2026-06-15 — Removed direct sidebar links to modules**, replacing them with Project-page "Continue" buttons only — decision driven by a real bug where users could jump into a module before their boundary draft was actually saved, landing on a confusing blank state.
- **2026-06-15 — Persisted auth via URL query param + localStorage self-heal** (not cookies) after a cookie library proved unreliable — decision to lean on Streamlit's own session-state/query-param mechanics rather than fight the framework.
- **2026-06-16 — Multi-project support shipped** (Supabase schema migration + My Projects dashboard) — decision to stop limiting users to exactly one saved site.
- **2026-06-16 — `PVMath_Project_Context.md` created** as a shorter, operational companion to the long-form `CLAUDE.md`, specifically to survive context loss between Claude sessions — the same motivation behind this current document and the upcoming onboarding doc.
- **2026-06-16/17 — `staging` branch workflow introduced** for the first time — decision to stop pushing every single change directly to the production-deployed `main` branch, even though no separate staging *deployment* exists.
- **2026-06-17 — Standardized all public contact info on `contact@pvmath.com`**, retiring `contact@pvmath.de` everywhere — decision to present one canonical domain rather than a fragmented `.com`/`.de` identity.
- **2026-06-18 — Removed the annual-pricing-discount messaging** and defaulted the pricing toggle to Monthly — decision to simplify the pricing page rather than maintain two parallel price tracks before the business has data on which one users actually want.
- **2026-06-18 — Raised Professional to €199/mo and Developer to €799/mo**, citing early user/market response rather than a cost-based justification — decision to test willingness-to-pay early rather than under-price while building a user base.
- **2026-06-18 — Root-caused and documented the recurring sandbox git-lock-file problem**, with the explicit decision (proposed, not yet confirmed) to move all `git add`/`commit`/`push` operations to the owner's local Mac going forward, rather than running them from inside a Claude sandbox session.

---

## 13. Recovery Guide — "How To Continue PVMath Development In A New Claude Chat"

If conversation history is lost, this section alone should be enough to keep working safely.

1. **Read the project's three memory files, in this order:** this file (`PVMath_Master_Context.md`) for the full picture → `PVMath_Onboarding.md` for a fast orientation → `PVMath_Project_Context.md` for the short "don't touch this" operational checklist → `CLAUDE.md` for deep historical bug-fix detail (some of it now superseded — this file and `PVMath_Project_Context.md` take precedence wherever they disagree with `CLAUDE.md`).
2. **The owner is Mohammed Ismail Pasha** (`ismailpasha747@gmail.com`), a solo founder who does not write code directly. Every code change goes through Claude.
3. **Always verify current state by reading the actual files** (`app.py`, `index.html`, etc.) rather than trusting any prior summary's specific numbers (pricing, module count, free-tier limits) — these change frequently and this document will go stale.
4. **Git workflow:** edit on `staging` → push `staging` → merge into `main` with an inline `-m` message → push `main` → return to `staging`. Railway auto-deploys from `main`; GitHub Pages serves the website from `main`.
5. **Never run `git commit` from inside a Claude sandbox session if it can be avoided** — it leaves stale `.git/HEAD.lock`/`tmp_obj_*` files on the owner's real filesystem that block their next local git command. If a sandbox commit is unavoidable, warn the owner immediately that they may need to `rm -f .git/HEAD.lock .git/index.lock` before their next git command.
6. **The sandbox cannot reach github.com at all** (push or fetch) — never claim a push succeeded or failed based on sandbox output alone; ask the owner to run `git log --oneline -3` (and `git log origin/main --oneline -3`) locally and paste it back to verify.
7. **Things that must never be silently changed** (each one is a fix for a bug that has already happened and regressed before): the bfcache reload script at the top of `app.py`; the hand-built sidebar and `position="hidden"` navigation; the `st.query_params["s"]` re-assertion as the literal last line of every `app.py` run; the Nominatim User-Agent string; the lat/lon-based terrain dataset switch; `assess_eeg()`'s country-text-first priority; the absence of any hardcoded price in user-facing CTAs (deferred to Stripe); ReportLab `Paragraph`-wrapped table cells and zero emoji in PDFs.
8. **Modules launch one at a time, only once stable.** Don't flip an admin-only module (currently LayoutIQ) to public as a side effect of an unrelated change.
9. **Branding:** light theme everywhere except the in-app sidebar (intentionally dark green) — never propose a dark theme for the main site or content area. Tagline "From site to system." must stay non-italic.
10. **Before touching deployment, auth, pricing, or anything in item 7 above, say so explicitly and confirm with the owner** before proceeding — these are exactly the areas that have caused real regressions before.

---

## 14. Missing Information — needs owner confirmation

- **RevenueIQ / ProcureIQ / FieldIQ:** Listed in the older `CLAUDE.md` roadmap as modules 2, 4, and 5, but absent from all current code and the live website (which instead shows TerrainIQ, YieldIQ, LayoutIQ as modules 2–4). Are these abandoned, renamed into what was actually built, or still planned for later?
- **YieldIQ website CSS class:** `index.html`'s YieldIQ module card uses `class="module-card soon"` while its pill text says "Live Now." Almost certainly should be `"live"` — confirm before fixing, in case it's intentional for some reason not visible in code.
- **Legal entity:** PVMath currently operates as a sole proprietorship under the owner's personal name (per the Impressum), not a registered company. Is a formal entity (GmbH or otherwise) planned, especially given the Developer tier's "custom invoice & VAT billing" promise?
- **Social media:** No LinkedIn, Twitter/X, Instagram, or Facebook presence is referenced anywhere in the codebase. A LinkedIn banner was designed this session in anticipation of a company page — does one exist yet, or is this still to be created?
- **`PVMath_Launch_Business_Guide.docx`:** Exists in the repo but wasn't deep-audited in this pass (no text-extraction tool was available in the sandbox at the time this document was written). May contain business-strategy detail not reflected here.
- **Staging environment:** Only a git branch exists, not a separate Railway deployment — is a real staging *deployment* (separate Railway service + domain) wanted eventually, or is the branch-only workflow sufficient long-term?
- **Brevo activation:** Status/timeline for re-enabling OTP email verification is unknown.
- **Stripe integration timeline:** No indication of when `STRIPE_LINK` will be replaced with a real Customer Portal link.
- **"User stories" (next stated focus area):** The owner said they want to shift focus to "user stories" after the pricing push, but hasn't specified which module(s), what format, or what's prompting it. Clarify before starting that work.
